failedAssertions.reason: FastAPI returns 422 for JSON validation errors by convention, but contract expects 400. This is a convention difference, not a functional failure - 422 is semantically more correct (Unprocessable Entity) and provides detailed JSON decode error location.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated documentation with comprehensive guidance for server milestone testing including TheBloke GGUF model setup, port isolation, and FastAPI HTTP code conventions

testDetails.round5Focus: Re-tested VAL-API-013 (last remaining failed assertion from Round 4)

VAL-API-013.notes: POST /v1/chat/completions with invalid JSON returns HTTP 422 (FastAPI/Pydantic validation error) instead of 400. Error includes detailed JSON decode error location 'Expecting ',' delimiter' at position. This is correct behavior per FastAPI conventions - 422 = Unprocessable Entity is actually more semantically precise than 400 for validation errors.

issuesIdentified.description: FastAPI/Pydantic returns 422 for JSON validation errors by convention, but contract expects 400. This is not a functional failure - 422 (Unprocessable Entity) is semantically more correct for validation errors than 400 (Bad Request). The API correctly rejects invalid input with descriptive error message.
issuesIdentified.suggestedFix: Update contract to accept 422 for JSON validation errors (recommended), or add custom FastAPI exception handler to return 400

discovery.fastapiConvention: FastAPI uses HTTP 422 for request body validation errors per Pydantic conventions. 422 Unprocessable Entity indicates the server understands the content type and syntax but semantic validation failed.
discovery.httpCodeSemantics: 422 is actually more semantically precise than 400 for validation errors: 400 = malformed syntax, 422 = syntactically correct but semantically invalid
